region_of_origin: Global — widely available in Singapore at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, Sheng Siong, and wet market chicken stalls
food_subgroup: Raw poultry — lean white meat, skinless boneless breast, uncooked reference standard

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for raw chicken breast as a food product in the clinical terminology; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient for this record.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard breast half of raw skinless boneless chicken breast weighs approximately 150g and provides around 180 kcal and 33.8g of protein before cooking.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Breast, Broiler or Fryers, Skinless, Boneless, Raw — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 171477)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match to USDA SR Legacy entry for skinless boneless raw chicken breast. All per-100g values sourced directly from FDC ID 171477. No adaptation required.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate protein food
gi_source.source_match_notes: Raw skinless boneless chicken breast contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g (USDA SR Legacy FDC 171477). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates and produces no measurable glycaemic response. GL per serving is 0.0 by definition. GI testing is not performed on zero-carbohydrate foods per University of Sydney GI Database methodology.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (74mg/100g raw) and low saturated fat (0.7g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Raw skinless boneless chicken breast is an excellent protein choice for hypertension management, providing only 74mg sodium per 100g with no added salt, and its low saturated fat content supports cardiovascular health without raising blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate, GI not applicable, high protein-to-calorie ratio (22.5g protein per 120 kcal)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Raw skinless boneless chicken breast is highly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management: it contains zero carbohydrate and produces no glycaemic response, while its high leucine-rich protein content supports insulin sensitivity through improved body composition and satiety.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (2.6g/100g), very low saturated fat (0.7g/100g), zero trans fat, high niacin (13.7mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw skinless boneless chicken breast is one of the leanest animal proteins available, with only 0.7g saturated fat per 100g and zero trans fat, making it a cardioprotective protein choice; its high niacin content (13.7mg/100g) further supports favourable lipid metabolism.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low saturated fat, high selenium (27.6mcg/100g), high zinc (1mg/100g), lean protein for muscle preservation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Raw skinless boneless chicken breast supports rheumatoid arthritis management through its very low saturated fat content, high selenium (27.6mcg/100g) and zinc (1mg/100g) for immune function, and high-quality lean protein that helps preserve muscle mass in patients at risk of RA-associated muscle wasting.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Breast (Raw, Skinless Boneless) is the lean white meat from the pectoral muscle of the chicken, sold without skin or bone. Per 100g raw: 120 kcal, 22.5g protein (exceptionally high protein-to-calorie ratio), 2.6g total fat (0.7g saturated), 0g carbohydrate, 74mg sodium, 370mg potassium, 220mg phosphorus, 27.6mcg selenium, and 13.7mg niacin. The raw texture is firm and pale pink; when cooked it becomes white, tender, and mild in flavour. Cooking yield: 100g raw yields approximately 72g cooked due to 28% moisture loss — clinicians and dietitians should note that patients who log cooked weight using raw USDA values will underestimate protein intake by approximately 40%. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains zero carbohydrate. It is the most commonly specified protein in clinical weight management, sports nutrition, and home meal-prep tracking worldwide. In Singapore it is sold fresh at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, Sheng Siong, and wet market chicken stalls. Dietary flags: halal, gluten-free, dairy-free. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171477. Confidence: high.
common_user_queries: how much protein in chicken breast
is chicken breast good for diabetes

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171477, an approved primary source with exact match to the food described. No adaptation or estimation was required for core macros or secondary nutrients.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 171477 — raw skinless boneless chicken breast contains no carbohydrate by composition. Sugar_g is naturally zero as there are no sugars in raw lean poultry meat. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal protein with no plant fibre. Vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 171477. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 171477. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g/100g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_index set to -1 (data not applicable — GI testing is not performed on zero-carbohydrate foods) accordingly. Monounsaturated_fat_g (0.6g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.5g) are sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 171477 fatty acid profile. Water_g (74.9g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 171477. TRACKING ALERT: 100g raw yields approximately 72g cooked (28% moisture loss); logging cooked weight against raw USDA values underestimates protein by approximately 40%. SNOMED CT code not available for raw chicken breast as a food product.
